components.title: Button
components.summary: A control that initiates an action when tapped, clicked, or activated.
components.summaryLong: The fundamental action control. Combine with a role, a button style (including the iOS 26 .glass / .glassProminent), tint, controlSize, buttonBorderShape, and buttonSizing to cover every native variant.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Text label. Prefer the title+systemImage initializer so the button can adapt icon/title rendering per container (toolbar, menu) and for VoiceOver.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol name. Optional. Enables iconOnly rendering in toolbars while preserving an accessibility title.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: .destructive renders red; .cancel/.confirm/.close gain semantic styling in dialogs and sheets. .confirm and .close are iOS 26+.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonStyle(_:). .glass and .glassProminent are iOS 26 Liquid Glass styles. .borderedProminent is the standard call-to-action.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). Drives accent for bordered/prominent/glass styles. Use design-system accent, never raw white/black.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Applied via .controlSize(_:). .extraLarge is iOS 26+. Affects padding and height across button-like controls.

properties.name: buttonBorderShape
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonBorderShape(_:). .circle is iOS 26+. Only affects bordered/glass styles.

properties.name: buttonSizing
properties.notes: iOS 26+. Applied via .buttonSizing(_:). .flexible expands to fill available width; .fitted hugs content.

properties.name: repeatBehavior
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonRepeatBehavior(_:). .enabled fires the action repeatedly while held. iOS 17+.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled). Disabled buttons dim and stop receiving input.

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .labelStyle(_:). .iconOnly keeps the title for VoiceOver while hiding it visually.

components.higNotes: Use a clear verb for the title. One prominent (call-to-action) button per screen region. Reserve .destructive red for irreversible actions and pair with a confirmation. Maintain a 44pt minimum tap target. On tvOS prefer .card style.

components.title: Button Role
components.summary: A semantic value describing a button's purpose, driving system styling.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: .destructive and .cancel are iOS 15+. .confirm and .close are iOS 26+ and gain glass-era semantic treatment in sheets and dialogs.

components.higNotes: Roles let the system place and color buttons correctly automatically. In a confirmationDialog, mark the dangerous option .destructive and the safe escape .cancel so the system orders and emphasizes them per platform conventions.

components.title: Menu
components.summary: A control that presents a menu of actions, optionally with a primary action.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Menu label. Can also be a Label with systemImage for icon+title.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: Optional SF Symbol for the menu's label.

properties.name: menuStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .menuStyle(_:). .button renders the menu trigger as a button; .borderlessButton (macOS) is borderless. Deprecated bordered/borderlessButton variants exist.

properties.name: hasPrimaryAction
properties.notes: When true, use the primaryAction: initializer: tap fires the primary action; menu opens on long-press / indicator click.

properties.name: useSections
properties.notes: Group items with Section to create visually separated blocks within the menu.

properties.name: useNestedMenu
properties.notes: Nest a Menu inside the content closure to create a submenu.

properties.name: menuOrder
properties.notes: Applied via .menuOrder(_:). .fixed keeps declared order top-to-bottom; .priority surfaces the first item nearest the trigger. iOS 16+.

properties.name: menuIndicator
properties.notes: Applied via .menuIndicator(_:). Controls the disclosure chevron. iOS 15+.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Applies when the menu adopts a button style.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Group related items with Section and dividers. Put the most common action first. Use a primaryAction when one action dominates but advanced options should stay one gesture away. Avoid deep nesting beyond one submenu level. Mark destructive items with role: .destructive.

components.title: Toggle
components.summary: A control that switches between on and off states bound to a Boolean.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Describes what the toggle controls. A second Text in the label closure becomes a subtitle.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: Optional SF Symbol via the title+systemImage initializer.

properties.name: isOn
properties.notes: The bound state. In a showroom, drive the live value directly.

properties.name: toggleStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .toggleStyle(_:). .switch is the iOS default; .button renders an on/off pill (great in toolbars); .checkbox is macOS-only.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). Colors the 'on' track of the switch and the filled state of the button style.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Most impactful on macOS and with the .button style.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use a toggle for an immediate, reversible binary setting (no Apply step). Label states clearly; the label should read as the thing being enabled, not as a question. The .button style suits toolbars; .checkbox belongs to macOS forms. Keep the 'on' tint consistent with the app accent.

components.title: Stepper
components.summary: A control that increments and decrements a value via plus/minus buttons.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Label shown beside the stepper controls.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Bound value. Use the value:in:step: initializer to clamp to a range.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: Bounds passed to in:. Buttons auto-disable at the limits.

properties.name: step
properties.notes: Increment per tap. Pass to step:.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Affects the +/- glyph footprint, most visible on macOS.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use a stepper for small, discrete adjustments where exact values matter and the range is narrow. For wide ranges prefer a Slider. Always show the current value next to the control. Not available on tvOS.

components.title: EditButton
components.summary: A button that toggles the editMode environment value for an editable container.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: Typically placed in a toolbar. The button auto-switches its title between Edit and Done.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Pair with a List/ForEach that declares onDelete and onMove so Edit mode reveals delete and reorder affordances. The button reflects state automatically (Edit ↔ Done) — never hardcode the title. iOS/iPadOS/Mac Catalyst only.

components.title: RenameButton
components.summary: A button that triggers a rename action registered via the renameAction modifier.

properties.name: context
properties.notes: Commonly placed in a contextMenu or toolbar. Activation calls the nearest .renameAction { } in the environment.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: RenameButton has no closure of its own — it dispatches to the enclosing .renameAction(_:). Use it so the system supplies the correct localized label and triggers inline rename (e.g. a focused TextField). Pair with @FocusState to begin editing.

components.title: PasteButton
components.summary: A system button that reads Transferable items from the pasteboard into a closure.

properties.name: payloadType
properties.notes: The Transferable type the button accepts; the closure receives an array of that type.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). One of the few customizations the system button allows.

properties.name: buttonBorderShape
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonBorderShape(_:).

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .labelStyle(_:). The label text is system-provided and localized.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: On iOS the button auto-validates/invalidates against pasteboard contents; on macOS it does not.

components.higNotes: Use PasteButton instead of a custom paste control so the system grants pasteboard access without a permission prompt (privacy-preserving paste). The system supplies the label and icon — do not relabel. iOS auto-disables it when the pasteboard has no matching content.

components.title: ControlGroup
components.summary: A container that lays out semantically related controls in a context-appropriate cluster.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Used when the group collapses into a toolbar overflow menu. Often a Label with systemImage.

properties.name: controlGroupStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .controlGroupStyle(_:). .palette shows a row of icon buttons; .compactMenu collapses into a single menu. .navigation/.compactMenu are iOS 16+.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Sizes the clustered controls together.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:) to color the grouped controls uniformly.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled) on the whole group.

components.higNotes: Group only controls that belong together (e.g. share + favorite + delete). The group adopts the right look per context — segmented in a toolbar, an overflow menu when space is tight. Provide a label so the overflow menu has a name. Not available on watchOS.

components.title: Link
components.summary: A control that navigates to a URL, opening it via the system or the app's openURL handler.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: The visible label for the link.

properties.name: destination
properties.notes: Target URL. Opens in the default handler unless intercepted by an openURL environment action.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Link is button-like and honors .buttonStyle(_:).

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:).

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use Link for navigation to external URLs, not for in-app routing (use a coordinator-driven Button for that). Override behavior app-wide with the openURL environment value to handle deep links.

components.title: ShareLink
components.summary: A button that presents the system share sheet for one or more Transferable items.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: The item(s) to share. Any Transferable: URL, String, Image, custom type.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Optional custom label. Default is a localized Share label with the share icon.

properties.name: subject
properties.notes: Optional subject line passed to share targets like Mail.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Optional default message body for the share sheet.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: ShareLink honors .buttonStyle(_:).

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Prefer ShareLink over presenting UIActivityViewController manually — it adapts the label per platform and supplies SharePreview for rich previews. Provide a SharePreview when sharing images or files so recipients see a thumbnail and title.

components.title: HelpLink
components.summary: A button with a system-standard help appearance (the circled question mark) that opens documentation relevant to its context. macOS-only.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Help-book anchor opened on activation. Use the anchor: initializer for documentation, or the action closure initializer for a custom action (open a URL / window).

properties.name: useAction
properties.notes: When true, use the HelpLink { } action initializer instead of anchor: to perform an arbitrary action such as openURL.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: HelpLink has a fixed standard appearance — do not relabel or restyle it. The system places it automatically: top-trailing in an alert or confirmationDialog, lower-leading in a sheet toolbar. macOS only; gate with #if os(macOS).

components.title: TextFieldLink
components.summary: A watchOS control that presents the system text-input interface (scribble / dictation / keyboard) when pressed and returns the entered text. watchOS-only.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Optional prompt shown in the text-input interface.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: The button label. Can also be a Label with systemImage.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use on watchOS to collect short text without a custom keyboard. The system supplies the input UI (dictation, scribble, emoji). watchOS only; gate with #if os(watchOS).

components.title: NewDocumentButton
components.summary: A button for the document launch experience that creates a new document, optionally via a template picker. Lives in a DocumentGroupLaunchScene. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Custom label text. Omit to get the system default "Create Document" label.

properties.name: usesTemplate
properties.notes: When true, use the for:Document.self initializer with an async closure to present a template picker before creating the document.

components.higNotes: Only valid inside a DocumentGroupLaunchScene. Provide one button for a blank document and optionally a second for a template chooser. iOS 18 / iPadOS 18 / macOS 15 / visionOS 2+.
